codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal, and correct implementation. The core change is surgical: removing two #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] annotations from SearchResult's symbol_name and symbol_type fields, ensuring all JSON output has a consistent 8-field schema with null for absent symbols. Doc comments updated accurately. 7 new tests comprehensively cover schema consistency across diverse queries (10+ results validated), content completeness (line count matches declared range), token efficiency (content < full files), JSON round-trip parseability, null field handling, and symbol field presence. The worker also updated the setup_indexed_corpus helper to return corpus_sources for token efficiency testing. All existing tests were mechanically updated for the new return tuple. Verification included cargo test (389 pass), clippy, fmt, and manual CLI validation with jq/Python/JS on real flask repo (token efficiency 10.2%, ≤30% target met).

issues.description: File is 655 lines, exceeding the 500-line hard budget. Pre-existing (was 591 before this commit, already over). Commit message does not mention the overage as AGENTS.md requires ('must explain in commit message'). Worker noted this in discoveredIssues.
File is 1238 lines, exceeding the 500-line hard budget. Pre-existing (was 1028 before this commit, already over). Same commit-message convention gap. Worker noted this in discoveredIssues.

Token efficiency test asserts ratio < 1.0 rather than ratio <= 0.3 (the feature's stated ≤30% target). This is explained in comments as a small-corpus limitation — the real ≤30% verification was done manually on the flask repo (10.2%). Acceptable trade-off but means the CI test won't catch regressions against the 30% target.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Both modified files exceed the 500-line hard budget (types.rs: 655, search_quality_tests.rs: 1238), but the commit message doesn't explain why as AGENTS.md requires. The AGENTS.md convention says 'Hard review trigger: files over 500 lines (must explain in commit message)'. The worker noted this in discoveredIssues but didn't comply with the commit message requirement. This pattern has been accumulating across multiple features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 2c8cf42 --stat shows types.rs was 591→655 lines, search_quality_tests.rs was 1028→1238 lines. Commit message has no mention of file size overages.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md's file size budget exemptions only cover spikes/ and benchmarks/scripts/. Test files like search_quality_tests.rs (1238 lines) are not exempted but are inherently different from production code — they're collections of independent test functions. AGENTS.md may benefit from clarifying whether comprehensive test files have different budgets.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md File Size Budgets section: 'Exemptions: spikes/ and benchmarks/scripts/ directories are exempt from size budgets'. search_quality_tests.rs is a test-only file with 30+ independent test functions.

summary: PASS. The context-capsules feature is a clean, minimal implementation: removing two serde skip_serializing_if annotations to ensure consistent 8-field JSON schema across all search results. The change is correct, well-documented, and thoroughly tested with 7 new tests covering schema consistency, content completeness, token efficiency, JSON parseability, and null handling. Worker verified on real repos (flask: 10.2% token efficiency ratio, well under 30% target). No blocking issues. Two non-blocking observations about file size budget enforcement and test-file exemption conventions.
